cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why Babbage failed to continue his research work in 1828. The options are A to D. First, I'll need to look at the passage to find the relevant part. The passage mentions that when Babbage returned to London in 1828, he was at the end of his resources. Many bills remained unpaid. His assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with many expensive tools. For one year, no work was done. So the key points here are that he had financial issues (end of his resources, unpaid bills) and conflicts with his team leading to them leaving. The question is why he failed to continue in 1828. The options are A: poor health, B: ran out of money, C: co-workers argued and left, D: spent money on bills. The passage says his health broke down earlier, in the four years he worked in London before 1828, leading to a holiday abroad. But in 1828 when he returned, the main issues were financial (end of resources) and the co-workers leaving. The question is about the main reason he couldn't continue. The answer options B and C are both mentioned. But the question says "mainly because", so which one is the primary cause? The text says "he was at the end of his resources. Many bills remained unpaid. His chief assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with many expensive tools." So the financial problem (B) led to co-workers leaving (C). The main reason would be the financial issues because that's what caused the unpaid bills and then the quarrels. So the main cause would be B. The answer is B. But wait, the options are (B) he almost ran out of money and (C) his co-workers argued with him. The question is why he failed to continue in 1828.
